name: ptouch-cradle
description: Two-part desktop cradle + label-catch tray for Brother PT-P750W label printer. Owl motif: ear tufts on tall back panel, owl face (eyes/pupils/beak) on tray front. Cradle has full-perimeter 25 mm low bathtub wall on all four sides with tall back panel rising to 145 mm body (180 mm with tufts). Parts: cradle and tray (kanban bin style, 100x91mm interior).

params.cable_slot_style: top-open notch (no bridge), resides entirely within low perimeter wall zone z=0-25mm
params.side_wall_scallops: REMOVED in Revision 6 — walls too short (25mm) for 10mm radius scallops
params.side_wall_eye_cutouts: REMOVED in Revision 6 — walls too short (25mm) for 10mm radius eye cutouts
params.ear_tuft_outer_edge: vertical, flush with exterior side face of back panel
params.label_drop_note: PT-P750W auto-cuts tape; labels exit at z=64-79mm desk-ref, clear front wall top at z=29mm desk-ref by 35-50mm, drop forward and down into tray.
params.tape_exit_position: lower-middle of front face, confirmed from product photography
params.usb_dc_ports_face: back (assumed, not confirmed in product photos)
params.tray_grip_scallop_location: top edge of tray front wall, centered

testPrintCandidates.reason: Tray exterior (103.2 mm W × 94.2 mm D × 41.6 mm H) must slide smoothly into cradle slot with 0.35 mm per-side clearance. If FDM dimensional error skews either part, tray will bind or rattle. Critical to verify fit quality before printing full parts.

Scoop face at 45° from horizontal is exactly at the FDM overhang threshold. Verify slicer does not add unnecessary support on the scoop face and that the surface quality is acceptable for a user-visible label-retrieval surface.
Tray walls at 1.6 mm (4 perimeters at 0.4 mm) are above minimum but thin. Verify wall integrity and that the tray body does not flex noticeably when handled.

Ear tufts peak at z = 180 mm with a 2 mm radius rounded apex. The apex is the topmost geometry and involves a perimeter that narrows to a ~4 mm wide shape at the top layers. Verify the slicer handles the narrow top gracefully (no stringing, clean tip) and that the 2 mm radius reads as intended rather than printing as a flat top.
